notes: Huge RMS + helios HBM-bound (~80-83% of 4.8 TB/s peak). Small shapes CPU launch/dispatch-bound (~32us; rmsnorm via register_custom_op + Triton).
RMS only; helios fell back. Superseded by normv2 (adds LN kernel).

correctness: 33/33; helios LN 2.86e-6 vs baseline & fp32 ref (within 1e-5)
ncu: rms_huge_cand 70.65% DRAM (1056 blocks) vs base 83.15%; ln_cand 74.22% DRAM (660 blocks, capped) vs base 79.73%
diagnosis: occupancy-capped grid-stride starved bandwidth on the big shapes (too few resident blocks -> low memory-level parallelism)
notes: Led to the launch-policy experiments below.

findings: normv3 (both uncapped, one-worker-per-row): helios 1.065x (LN DRAM 660->8640 blocks lifted to 79.8%), but huge RMS dropped to 1.00x (81090 blocks added launch/tail overhead for tiny-per-row work). normv4 (RMS capped + LN uncapped): all shapes >=1.02x, geomean 1.408x. Conclusion: LN (heavy per-block) wants one-CTA-per-row; RMS (tiny per-row) wants persistent capped grid-stride.

finding: [P2] docs/sglang_export.patch enabled the H200-tuned native kernels by DEFAULT on any CUDA host where the JIT build succeeds (the kda_kernels package is arch-dispatched, but the upstream in-tree patch bypassed that protection)
fix: both _can_use_native_* gates in the patched files now require torch.cuda.get_device_capability() == (9, 0) (inside the try block, before the jit build) in addition to the env toggle; patch regenerated from the updated worktree. kda_kernels package unchanged (already arch-gated by _dispatcher).
validation: on H200 (CC 9.0): gates True with toggles on (behavior unchanged); spot off/on arbiter pair consistent with the round-0 official pairs (official geomean stays 1.4458x from the 2-pair round-0 measurement); all four probes pass (fp16/non-contig gate False + ref-equal, is_rms_norm bypass, torch.compile bitwise). Gate cost: @cache_once static (one capability query per process), zero steady-state per-call cost.
evidence: docs/sglang_export.patch (2 hits of the capability check); REMOTE_KDA_DIR/arbiter_{off,on}_r2.json

finding: [P2] the round-2 arch gate checked get_device_capability() for the CURRENT device and cached by dim only; in heterogeneous multi-GPU processes the native path could enable on an unvalidated device or stay off for an H200 tensor
fix: _can_use_native_ln_infer(n, device_index) / _can_use_native_one_pass_rms(dim, device_index): capability checked for the TENSOR's device (callsites pass x.device.index) and the cache_once key includes the device index (per-device decisions). bench_intree_arbiter.py gate asserts updated to the new signature. Patch regenerated.
validation: H200 GPU0: capability(0)==(9,0); gates True (toggle on) / False (off); end-to-end tensor call routes through the device-keyed gate; spot off/on arbiter pair consistent with round-0 official pairs (official geomean stays 1.4458x); all four probes pass

finding: [P2] the gates checked the tensor device's capability but built via load_jit under the CURRENT device's JIT arch selection - in heterogeneous processes a cubin could compile for the wrong architecture before launching on the H200 tensor device
fix: the gate's module build is wrapped in `with torch.cuda.device(device_index):` in both gates (first compilation - the only one - now happens with the tensor's device current; later calls hit the jit cache). Patch regenerated; export doc aligned.
validation: H200 GPU0: gates True; e2e rms+ln calls through device-context-built modules OK; quick off/on pair consistent with prior rounds (official geomean stays 1.4458x); all four probes pass. No-op on single-device pinned runs (device 0 already current).
